answer:
Salesforce Commerce Cloud does not offer a free tier [1]. All editions, including B2C and B2B versions, require custom pricing based on your business needs, often involving a percentage of Gross Merchandise Value [2][3][4].

Regarding developer access, while you can manage sandbox environments using a self-service API, this is not free [5][6][7]. To use on-demand sandboxes, you must purchase sandbox credits through a sales representative [6][7]. Once credits are purchased, you can provision and manage sandboxes programmatically using the Sandbox API [6][7].

Example for creating a sandbox via the Sandbox API:

```http
POST /sandboxes
Authorization: Bearer <your_access_token>
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "resource_profile": "medium",
  "ttl": 24
}
```

You must configure an API client ID in Account Manager and assign the Sandbox API User role to perform these actions [5][7].
